title: Serbian Football Star Files Police Complaint Over Former Chief's Intimidation Plot
content:
Serbian football star Nemanja Matić has filed a criminal complaint against Slaviša Kokeza, the former president of the Football Association of Serbia (FSS), following revelations that the official plotted to threaten and intimidate him.

Matić confirmed to OCCRP’s Serbian partner KRIK that he filed the complaint for "endangering safety" two weeks ago at a police station in his hometown of Ub. He declined to comment further on the case.

The move comes after a June 30 investigation by OCCRP and KRIK revealed that Kokeza used the encrypted Sky ECC app to discuss hiring mafia figures to assault a group of high-profile players. The targets included Matić, Danko Lazović, and Manchester United legend Nemanja Vidić, who had publicly criticized Kokeza's leadership.

According to the leaked 2020 messages, Kokeza used mob-style language and asked contacts to send people to intimidate the players' inner circles.

"Right now I realistically need the most help to eliminate Vidić, [Danko] Lazović, and Matić," he told one interlocutor.
snippet: Serbian football star Nemanja Matić has filed a criminal complaint against Slaviša Kokeza, the former president of the Football Association of Serbia (FSS), following revelations that the official
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: UK Brings New Trafficking Charges Against Tate Brothers
content:
Britain’s Crown Prosecution Service has brought 38 additional charges against social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate, including rape and trafficking for sexual exploitation, and requested their extradition from the United States.

The brothers were arrested by the U.S. Marshals Service on Saturday, July 18, according to a ⁠CPS statement published the following day. British prosecutors said the new charges concern four additional alleged victims and bring the total number of alleged victims in the U.K. case to seven.

Andrew Tate, 39, faces seven additional rape counts, three counts of arranging or facilitating trafficking for sexual exploitation, and three counts of assault causing actual bodily harm. He also faces 19 additional charges involving indecent images of a child and extreme pornography.

Tristan Tate, 38, faces two rape counts, one count of sexual assault, and three counts of arranging or facilitating trafficking for sexual exploitation.
snippet: Britain’s Crown Prosecution Service has brought 38 additional charges against social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate, including rape and trafficking for sexual exploitation, and requested
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Russian Security Service Planned AI Tool to Track Media
content:
Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) had been developing an artificial intelligence system designed to merge public media monitoring with restricted databases to help officers search for information about people and events, according to an investigation by OCCRP partner IStories. 

The project was never delivered, but leaked documents show how the FSB planned to use Artificial Intelligence technology developed by U.S. companies in its intelligence gathering operations.   

The documents indicate that a little-known technology company in Kazan called Micord worked on the system, named PAUK (spider) — a Russian acronym for “Portal for Analytics and Usage of Contents.” The internal documents were obtained after hackers breached Micord’s infrastructure in December 2025 and passed the material to the rights group Idite Lesom, which shared it with IStories.

The same leak was used in earlier IStories investigations into Russia’s digital military registry and other classified Defense Ministry systems.
snippet: Russia’s Federal Security Service (FSB) had been developing an artificial intelligence system designed to merge public media monitoring with restricted databases to help officers search for
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Breaking the Bloodline: Italy Enacts Landmark Law to Help Children Flee the Mafia
content:
Italy’s Parliament has approved landmark legislation establishing a nationwide network of protections for children and mothers seeking to defect from the country’s powerful mafia clans, formalizing a judicial strategy aimed at breaking the generational cycle of organized crime.

The Italian Senate unanimously passed the bill, known as “Free to Choose” (Liberi di Scegliere), on Wednesday, closing critical legal loopholes that had historically left families members fleeing criminal syndicates exposed to reprisals. 

The passage marks the final step in a legislative push that began in the lower house earlier this year.

The new law transforms a localized, experimental judicial protocol into a well-funded national program. It scales up an initiative launched in 2012 by Roberto Di Bella, a juvenile judge in Calabria—the southern region that serves as the power base for the ’Ndrangheta, currently Italy's richest and most dangerous global narcotics syndicate.

For over a decade, Judge Di Bella’s program has quietly relocated children—and the mothers who choose to accompany them—from southern strongholds like Calabria and Sicily to confidential locations in northern and central Italy.
snippet: Italy’s Parliament has approved landmark legislation establishing a nationwide network of protections for children and mothers seeking to defect from the country’s powerful mafia clans, formalizing a
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Ukrainian Media Defy Court Ban to Publish Spiked Corruption Probe
content:
In a dramatic show of journalistic solidarity, eight of Ukraine's leading news outlets on Friday defied a court-ordered injunction, jointly publishing a blocked investigative report into the family of a prominent law enforcement official.

The participating outlets issued a blistering joint statement that condemned the judicial gag order as an act of blatant state censorship.

The original report, produced by OCCRP’s Ukrainian partner Slidstvo.Info and the Anti-Corruption Action Center (AntAC), details the acquisition of 143 real estate properties in Kharkiv by the brother of Oleksandr Sukhachov, the director of Ukraine’s State Bureau of Investigation (SBI).

On July 6, Kyiv’s Pecherskyi District Court prohibited Slidstvo.Info, AntAC, and journalist Alina Stryzhak from disclosing information about the properties. The preemptive ban, issued before the investigation was even released, sparked a public outcry and condemnation from Ukrainian and international civic organizations.
snippet: In a dramatic show of journalistic solidarity, eight of Ukraine's leading news outlets on Friday defied a court-ordered injunction, jointly publishing a blocked investigative report into the family of
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: What It Means To Be Sicilian
content:
It was an unexpectedly sunny day in Belgium when my father called.

“Erika, he’s dead. Is the newspaper you work for reporting this? This is important.”

Dad doesn’t often call, he’s more of a texter, and I was surprised by the sudden urgency in his voice. 

“You mean Nitto Santapaola?” I asked, slightly confused. 

News of Sicilian mafia boss Benedetto “Nitto” Santapaola dying in custody at the age of 87 was making headlines around the world on that day in March of this year. The Cosa Nostra leader had passed away in a Milan prison while serving multiple life sentences. 

Santapaola’s power base had been in Catania in eastern Sicily, where my family is from. In that sense his death felt close to home. 

Although he was already in jail when I was growing up, it was a name that everybody knew but few dared to say aloud. Parents would warn their children in hushed tones not to associate with anyone who carried it. In Sicily, conversations about the mafia happen in lowered voices, whispered inside homes. We rarely name who we are actually talking about. “That one” is usually enough.
snippet:
It was an unexpectedly sunny day in Belgium when my father called.

“Erika, he’s dead. Is the newspaper you work for reporting this? This is important.”
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Company Managed by Relative of Dutch ‘Fat King’ Was Raided in Biofuel Fraud Investigation
content:
Six years after a Dutch court convicted the so-called "Fat King," Cornelis Bunschoten, in a major biodiesel scam case, a company managed by his relative is under investigation for a similar alleged fraud.

In November 2024, the environmental unit of the Rotterdam police raided the premises of a company in the Veluwe, a forested region in the east of the Netherlands. Police said the company was suspected of involvement in “a million euro fraud involving raw materials for biodiesel.”

Police didn’t name the company or its owner in their statement at the time. But OCCRP’s partner in the Netherlands, Follow The Money, has confirmed that the company is called Gerbuvet B.V., and it’s managed by Gerrit Bunschoten. 

Bunschoten is not under investigation. He did not respond to emailed questions about the case, and declined to comment when contacted by phone.

Gerrit is a family member of Cornelis “Cees” Bunschoten who was dubbed in Dutch media as the “vetkoning,” or “Fat King,” in reference to his business of refining cooking fats and other oils into biofuel, which reportedly made him one of the 500 richest people in the Netherlands.
snippet: Six years after a Dutch court convicted the so-called "Fat King," Cornelis Bunschoten, in a major biodiesel scam case, a company managed by his relative is under investigation for a similar alleged
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: European Password Manager Shares Origins and Updates with State-Certified Russian Firm
content:
From Irish government agencies to the Dresden University of Technology, the password manager Passwork Europe S.L. boasts a range of high-profile European clients who use its software to store the digital keys to their organizations.

The firm, whose software allows customers to host this data on private servers, repeatedly emphasizes its European credentials, marketing itself online as a Finnish-born enterprise that relocated its headquarters to Spain two years ago.

“Passwork - European company built for trust,” the English-language website states alongside a badge reading “Made in EU 2017.”

An online guide detailing how AI systems should describe the company, which Passwork released earlier this year, left no room for ambiguity:

“It is bootstrapped, founder-owned, and has no affiliations with any US, Russian, or other non-European entities,” the instructions said. 

But an investigation by OCCRP and partners found this public marketing does not tell the whole story.

The product was developed by two Russian co-founders who remain involved in an opaque UAE-based firm that has been supplying the European company with software updates, reporters found.
snippet: From Irish government agencies to the Dresden University of Technology, the password manager Passwork Europe S.L. boasts a range of high-profile European clients who use its software to store the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Irish Alumina Exports to Russia Revised Down, But Still Lead
content:
Ireland’s Central Statistics Office ⁠revised external trade data for alumina exports, but the updated figures still show Russia as the largest individual destination for shipments from the country’s only alumina producer, the Rusal-owned Aughinish Alumina refinery.

The new figures show that 50.25 percent of Aughinish Alumina’s exports went to Russia in the first quarter of 2026, while 39.22 percent went to the EU, according to figures reviewed by ⁠The Irish Times. The remainder went to other non-EU countries.

In 2025, 43 percent of Irish alumina exports — 775,985 tonnes — went to Russia, while 38.3 percent, or 689,785 tonnes, went to the EU. Earlier CSO figures had put the Russia share at 66.8 percent for 2025.

The revision follows questions over the original data. In May, CSO figures showed that 83 percent of alumina exports in the first quarter of 2026 had gone to Russia, while only 0.6 percent went to the EU. Government ministers later disputed those figures, saying Aughinish had told them that 45 percent of exports went to Russia. Aughinish subsequently told The Irish Times it had inadvertently provided incorrect figures to the CSO because of a “clerical error.”
snippet: Ireland’s Central Statistics Office revised external trade data for alumina exports, but the updated figures still show Russia as the largest individual destination for shipments from the country’s
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Piece by Piece: Iraq Seizes More Gold and Cash in Relentless Oil Graft Probe
content:
Iraqi investigators on Thursday continued their relentless sweep of properties tied to the country’s former deputy oil minister, uncovering another cache of wealth—including four kilograms of gold jewelry and $200,000 in cash—as the historic corruption investigation into Adnan al-Jumaili deepens.

The latest raid comes on the heels of a blockbuster announcement by judicial officials earlier in the week, when they revealed the recovery of 375 kilograms of gold alongside a network of commercial real estate, industrial factories, and cargo fleets valued at an estimated $47 million.

The multi-million-dollar enforcement action marks a dramatic escalation in the prosecution of the former Deputy Minister of Oil for Refining Affairs, who remains in custody on charges of systemic corruption and money laundering.
snippet: Iraqi investigators on Thursday continued their relentless sweep of properties tied to the country’s former deputy oil minister, uncovering another cache of wealth—including four kilograms of gold
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Hitmen Name Former Minister and Chief of Staff in Daphne Caruana Galizia Murder
content:
The two brothers convicted of assassinating Maltese investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia—Alfred and George Degiorgio—named Keith Schembri, the former chief of staff to then-Prime Minister Joseph Muscat, and former government minister Chris Cardona as being behind the plot.

The explosive claims came during their testimonies in the trial of businessman Yorgen Fenech, which began July 1, in Valletta. Fenech, the former head of the Tumas Group conglomerate, is accused of masterminding the murder. He was arrested in November 2019 allegedly fleeing the country on his yacht, though he denies involvement and has pleaded not guilty.

Caruana Galizia, 53, was murdered on October 16, 2017, near her home in Bidnija. A bomb placed under her car seat was remotely detonated by SMS.
snippet: The two brothers convicted of assassinating Maltese investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia—Alfred and George Degiorgio—named Keith Schembri, the former chief of staff to then-Prime Minister
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: U.K. Prosecutors Freeze $33M London Mansion
content:
British prosecutors have frozen a $32.7-million London mansion owned by a Chinese man with Cambodian citizenship on suspicion the property was purchased with illicit funds. 

The individual, Yu Cong, has not been charged with any crime in the U.K.  

The Proceeds of Crime unit of the U.K. Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) submitted documents to the land registry on July 3 showing the freeze on a mansion Yu Cong purchased in July 2024. The freeze has not been previously made public, and the documents were obtained by Transparency International U.K. and shared with OCCRP.

The CPS did not respond to a request for further information about the nature of its investigation, and about the origin of the funds used to buy the mansion. 

The mansion in London’s exclusive Highgate neighbourhood was previously owned by Nurali Aliyev, the grandson of Kazakhstan’s former president. 

The property had been frozen by the National Crime Agency (NCA) in 2019, which filed an “unexplained wealth order” against Aliyev. The order and the asset freeze were overturned in court the following year, OCCRP reported.
snippet: British prosecutors have frozen a $32.7-million London mansion owned by a Chinese man with Cambodian citizenship on suspicion the property was purchased with illicit funds.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Moroccan Government Used Powerful Israeli Pegasus Spyware, Former Intelligence Officer Says
content:
The Israeli-made software known as Pegasus is one of the most powerful spyware tools in the world. It can be deployed to infect a smartphone without the owner doing anything – no need to trick the victim into clicking a malicious link or downloading a compromised file. Once inside, it sees every file, message, photo, and call, and can even use the microphone and camera to secretly record the user.

The Israeli cyber-intelligence company behind the software, NSO Group, has long maintained that the sale, distribution, and use of Pegasus is tightly regulated. Export licenses for Pegasus are strictly controlled by the Israeli defense ministry, and the company asserts that it sells exclusively to vetted government clients to combat terrorism and serious crime. 

That narrative was shattered by the 2021 publication of the Pegasus Project, an investigation coordinated by the Paris-based nonprofit Forbidden Stories and Amnesty International, alongside media partners, including OCCRP. The landmark reporting revealed that the spyware had been systematically deployed worldwide against journalists, human rights activists, and politicians.
snippet: The Israeli-made software known as Pegasus is one of the most powerful spyware tools in the world. It can be deployed to infect a smartphone without the owner doing anything – no need to trick the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Nepal Court Jails Ex-Ministers in Refugee Fraud Case
content:
A District Court in Kathmandu sentenced former Nepalese Deputy Prime Minister Top Bahadur Rayamajhi to four years in prison and fined him 40,000 Nepalese rupees ($260) for his role in a scheme to fraudulently resettle Nepali citizens in the United States as Bhutanese refugees.

Former Home Minister Bal Krishna Khand received a two-year prison term and a fine of 20,000 rupees ($130).

Judge Tej Bahadur Khadka convicted 15 defendants of fraud, organized crime, and crimes against the state. One additional defendant was convicted of fraud. Prosecutors filed the case in May 2023.

Tek Narayan Pandey, who was home secretary at the time of the alleged scheme, was also sentenced to four years in prison and fined 40,000 rupees ($260).

Shiva Khatiwada, an information officer at the Kathmandu District Court, told OCCRP on Wednesday that the full text of the verdict would be available within seven to 10 days.

“Usually, the court's practice is to provide it within 21 days, but it will likely be available sooner,” Khatiwada said.
snippet: A District Court in Kathmandu sentenced former Nepalese Deputy Prime Minister Top Bahadur Rayamajhi to four years in prison and fined him 40,000 Nepalese rupees ($260) for his role in a scheme to
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Sanctioned Alleged Iranian Oil Network “Financier” Behind £25M in Luxury London Real Estate
content:
An Iranian businessman sanctioned by the U.S. this week for allegedly financing a massive oil-smuggling network owns 25 million British pounds ($33.46 million) in luxury London real estate, newly uncovered property records show.

On Tuesday, the U.S. Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned Iranian businessman Hossein Ghorbani Zahed as part of a broader package of measures targeting the “illicit shipping and sanctions evasion network” of oil distribution magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani. 

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the action against the Shamkhani network, first sanctioned in July 2025, was part of “ongoing efforts to ramp up economic pressure on the Iranian regime after it resumed destabilizing attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.”

Hossein Shamkhani is the son of Ali Shamkhani, a political adviser to Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. His father was killed in U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran in late February that also claimed the lives of Khamenei and other top officials.

Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani previously told Bloomberg that he does not own any oil firms, and only operates in countries “not under sanctions.”
snippet: An Iranian businessman sanctioned by the U.S. this week for allegedly financing a massive oil-smuggling network owns 25 million British pounds ($33.46 million) in luxury London real estate, newly
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Wanted in China with $100M in Properties Frozen in the UK, ‘Mr. X’ is Living in Japan
content:
He is wanted in China for allegedly running illegal gambling operations, and the U.K. has frozen more than $100-million worth of his London real estate. 

But for the past two years, Su Jiangbo has been living in Japan where he runs a travel business and owns two properties, OCCRP and Kyodo News have discovered.

Land and company records obtained by Kyodo News show that Su Jiangbo purchased a house in his name in the suburbs of Osaka in 2024. He is also listed as the director of an Osaka-based tourism and real estate company, which owns another house.

“I chose to relocate to Japan because of its political and economic stability, well-developed infrastructure, robust intellectual property protection, and welcoming, civilised society,” Su Jiangbo told OCCRP in an email.

Those factors, he added, “provide a strong foundation for long-term business development.” 

In the U.K., the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) issued freezing orders on his 85 London properties, but referred to Su Jiangbo in its March announcement only as “Mr. X.”
snippet: He is wanted in China for allegedly running illegal gambling operations, and the U.K. has frozen more than $100-million worth of his London real estate.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Sanctions Evader Zarrab Avoids Prison After Aiding U.S. Prosecutors
content:
A federal judge in New York on Tuesday allowed notorious money launderer Reza Zarrab to escape a federal jail term, more than eight years after the wealthy financier pleaded guilty to helping Iran evade U.S. sanctions.

Zarrab’s defense team, in a sentencing memorandum to the court, urged U.S. District Judge Richard M. Berman to set Zarrab free. They noted that since pleading guilty in 2017 he has helped prosecutors as a witness in the prosecution of Turkiye Halk Bankasi, A.S., a Turkish lender known as Halkbank.

The judge agreed, and waived any forfeiture because defense lawyers argued that Zarrab’s net worth was more than negative $50 million. Prosecutors had asked for a downward departure in sentencing, not giving a specific number but signaling they were not opposed to time served.

Dressed in a conservative suit, a somber Zarrab thanked the judge and said he appreciated the U.S. government and law enforcement officials for his safety and protection.
snippet: A federal judge in New York on Tuesday allowed notorious money launderer Reza Zarrab to escape a federal jail term, more than eight years after the wealthy financier pleaded guilty to helping Iran
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Spies, Malware, and Blackouts: EU Blames Secret FSB Unit for Decade of Cyber Sabotage
content:
The European Union and Britain on Monday blamed a highly specialized unit of Russia’s Federal Security Service, or FSB, for a decade-long campaign of cyber-espionage and attempted sabotage targeting critical infrastructure, including power grids and defense networks, across Europe.

To counter the threat, the Council of the European Union imposed asset freezes and travel bans on nine individuals and four entities linked to the campaign. Britain issued parallel sanctions targeting 24 individuals and entities.

In a coordinated series of announcements, Western officials identified the 16th Center of the FSB as the orchestrator of several notorious hacking collectives, including the group known as Turla. The unit’s digital fingerprints have been traced to government networks and essential services across nearly a dozen European nations, including France, Germany, Poland, and Ukraine. However, the 16th Center itself was not added to this specific sanctions list.

The attribution marks a rare public unmasking of Russia’s state hacking apparatus.
snippet: The European Union and Britain on Monday blamed a highly specialized unit of Russia’s Federal Security Service, or FSB, for a decade-long campaign of cyber-espionage and attempted sabotage targeting
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: U.S. Man Extradited for Smuggling Drugs Through Subterranean Border Railway
content:
Mexico extradited a United States citizen accused of orchestrating a sophisticated, cross-border drug smuggling operation through a subterranean railway tunnel, the Department of Justice announced.

Federal prosecutors describe Genaro Lopez as a “powerful drug trafficker” operating on both sides of the southern border. He pleaded not guilty in a federal court on Monday. If convicted of the narcotics conspiracy charges, Lopez faces a maximum penalty of life in prison.

At the center of the government’s sprawling case is a highly engineered, 532-meters passage discovered by federal agents in May 2022. Plunging over 18 meters below the surface, the tunnel was fully equipped with reinforced walls, electricity, ventilation, and a custom rail system.

The underground pipeline linked a property in Tijuana, Mexico, directly to a commercial warehouse in the Otay Mesa neighborhood of San Diego, a major industrial transit hub along the U.S.-Mexico border heavily trafficked by cargo trucks.
snippet: Mexico extradited a United States citizen accused of orchestrating a sophisticated, cross-border drug smuggling operation through a subterranean railway tunnel, the Department of Justice announced.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Graft, Gold, and Blackouts: Indonesia Prosecutes Its Own in $278 Million Coal Scandal
content:
Indonesian police on Monday transferred the criminal case file of Febrie Adriansyah, the former Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes, to the Attorney General’s Office for formal prosecution, the state news agency Antara reported.

The file transfer comes after authorities on Saturday formally named Adriansyah a suspect in a sweeping corruption and money laundering investigation that is part of a much larger, highly sensitive investigation launched in the wake of crippling, widespread power outages that plunged large swaths of the country into darkness in June.

Central to the probe are suspected procurement irregularities involving critical coal supplies intended for state-owned power plants. Investigators allege that the scheme caused state losses of approximately 5 trillion rupiah—nearly $278 million—and directly starved the energy sector of fuel, triggering the widespread rolling blackouts that have recently plagued the country.

With the file officially transferred, state prosecutors are expected to issue a new investigation warrant targeting their former colleague, according to Tempo, an Indonesian investigative outlet and partner of OCCRP.
snippet: Indonesian police on Monday transferred the criminal case file of Febrie Adriansyah, the former Deputy Attorney General for Special Crimes, to the Attorney General’s Office for formal prosecution, the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Critics Slam Albania’s Emergency Bailout of Kanye West Concert as a $5 Million Smoke Screen
content:
Critics accuse Prime Minister Edi Rama’s government of turning a publicly funded Ye (former Kanye West) concert on Saturday into a political spectacle that could help overshadow weeks of anti-government protests in Tirana.

The government approved 400 million lek, about $4.9 million, to help finance the July 11 concert by Ye. The money will come from the state reserve fund and be transferred to the Ministry of Tourism, Culture and Sports for what the government describes as a cultural-touristic event aimed at promoting Albania’s international image.

But Albanian writer and public commentator Fatos Lubonja has told OCCRP’s partner Shteg.org that the decision fits what he described as a “bread and circus” model of government: spending public money on entertainment while the country faces deeper problems.

“Perhaps he is also doing it to counter this protest, to stage a big show so people can say, ‘Look how many people gathered there,’” Lubonja told Shteg.org.
snippet: Critics accuse Prime Minister Edi Rama’s government of turning a publicly funded Ye (former Kanye West) concert on Saturday into a political spectacle that could help overshadow weeks of
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Two on Trial for Killing Ukrainian Woman Suspected of Planting a Bomb that Injured a Businessman in Monaco
content:
Ukrainian prosecutors accused two men of executing a woman who was the prime suspect in a recent luxury apartment bombing in Monaco that allegedly targeted a sanctioned Ukrainian businessman.

The details disclosed in a Kyiv court on Thursday appear to be just one piece of a broader, deeply mysterious plot. 

Prosecutors allege that Vladislav Reut, an employee of Ukraine’s Main Intelligence Directorate (HUR), and Vitaliy Zhykovych, a former officer of the Security Service of Ukraine (SBU), lured 39-year-old Anastasia Berezovskaya into a forest outside Kyiv and killed her. Both men were arrested, though a definitive motive for the murder has not yet been established.

According to Interpol, Berezovskaya was the central figure in last week’s assassination attempt in Monaco, where she allegedly planted an explosive device in the lobby of a residential building and detonated it remotely, severely injuring a man, a woman, and a child. 

While officials have not publicly released the names of the victims, a lawyer representing the family of Vadym Yermolaiev—a prominent Ukrainian businessman—confirmed that his client was among those injured and that he remains hospitalized in critical condition.
snippet: Ukrainian prosecutors accused two men of executing a woman who was the prime suspect in a recent luxury apartment bombing in Monaco that allegedly targeted a sanctioned Ukrainian businessman.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Millions in a Storm Drain: Iraq Cracks Open 'Integrated Network' in Massive Oil Scandal
content:
Iraqi authorities seized more than $10 million in local currency hidden inside a rainwater drainage pit, marking the latest discovery in an ongoing corruption investigation into the country's former Deputy Minister of Oil for Refining Affairs, Adnan al-Jumaili.

The Thursday bust follows a raid days earlier at al-Jumaili’s home in Tikrit, in north-central Iraq, where judicial authorities uncovered 25 billion Iraqi dinars (approximately $19.09 million), $1 million in U.S. currency, and roughly five kilograms of gold. 

These were just the latest seizures of illicit funds that stemmed from systemic abuses and kickbacks tied to projects overseen by the former official, according to Iraq's Supreme Judicial Council. The latest drainage-pit discovery brings the total value of assets seized during the probe to 127 billion Iraqi dinars (approximately $96.9 million) and $24 million.

Bassem Haidar Kadhem al-Gharabi, a member of Iraq's parliamentary Finance Committee, told OCCRP on Friday that the massive scale of the cache points to "an integrated network" rather than a lone actor.
snippet: Iraqi authorities seized more than $10 million in local currency hidden inside a rainwater drainage pit, marking the latest discovery in an ongoing corruption investigation into the country's former
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Ukraine Accuses Russian Prison Doctor of War Crimes Following OCCRP/Schemes Investigation
content:
Ukrainian authorities on Friday officially charged a Russian prison doctor and an inmate accomplice with war crimes, detailing a horrific campaign of torture, sexual violence, and medical abuse against Ukrainian prisoners of war and abducted civilians.

The formal Notices of Suspicion issued by the Security Service of Ukraine (SBU) follow a joint investigation published Thursday by OCCRP and Schemes, which exposed systematic abuse at Correctional Colony No. 7 (IK-7) in Russia's Vladimir region.

Reporters from Schemes shared their findings with Ukrainian law enforcement agencies, helping investigators identify the perpetrators. The journalistic investigation drew on interviews with about 50 captives who returned from the colony between 2024 and May 2026. Multiple survivors identified the prison doctor, known to them by the nickname "Konoval" (The Quack), as 48-year-old Vyacheslav Cherdantsev.
snippet: Ukrainian authorities on Friday officially charged a Russian prison doctor and an inmate accomplice with war crimes, detailing a horrific campaign of torture, sexual violence, and medical abuse
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Seven-Country Operation Nets 28 Child Abuse Suspects
content:
Authorities across seven countries have rescued three children and arrested 28 people in a Europol-coordinated operation targeting child sexual exploitation.

Led by Norway, the investigation ran from late May to mid-June and involved law enforcement in Canada, Czechia, Germany, Norway, Poland, Sweden, and Switzerland. Through cryptocurrency tracing, investigators also identified two alleged sellers of child sexual abuse material.

Authorities believe the suspects used digital currency to purchase access to dark web forums where videos of child abuse were available for download or streaming. Officers seized more than 460 items during the raids, including electronic devices, crypto wallets, narcotics, and large quantities of doping substances.

According to officials, one suspect allegedly used artificial intelligence to generate illicit material, while some victims were identified as immediate family members of another suspect. The investigation remains ongoing, and authorities expect further arrests.
snippet: Authorities across seven countries have rescued three children and arrested 28 people in a Europol-coordinated operation targeting child sexual exploitation.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Interpol: 5,811 Arrested, $293M Seized in Massive Cyber Crackdown
content:
An international anti-fraud operation coordinated by Interpol has led to the arrest of 5,811 individuals and the interception of $293 million in illicit assets, the international police agency announced Thursday. The operation, which spanned 97 countries between Jan. 15 and April 30, targeted ‘social engineering’ schemes. These included business email compromise, sextortion, impersonation, and investment scams, as well as the money laundering activities linked to them.

Over the course of the four-month crackdown, authorities identified more than 142,000 victims globally across 152,808 cases. Interpol stated that the sheer number of victims “[highlights] the extent to which social engineering scams and fraud have escalated into a major transnational threat.”

In Eswatini, police arrested 82 suspects and dismantled a criminal network running illegal online gambling, money laundering, and impersonation scams. Meanwhile, authorities in Palau deported 22 individuals for their roles in two connected scam centers operating out of hotels.
snippet: An international anti-fraud operation coordinated by Interpol has led to the arrest of 5,811 individuals and the interception of $293 million in illicit assets, the international police agency
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Former Ukrainian Captives Tell of Torment at Hands of Russian Prison Doctor
content:
In the summer of 2023, a virulent scabies epidemic broke out in Prison Colony No. 7, deep in the Russian heartland.

The infestation, caused by mites that trigger intense itching and a red rash, took an especially serious turn for Ihor Shyshko, a Ukrainian prisoner of war. 

“It was all spreading into my eyes,” he said, recalling his decision to try to obtain medical treatment. “I thought I should ask, maybe they’d give me brilliant green [antiseptic dye], maybe I’d smear it on, maybe it would somehow stop it.”

Instead, Shyshko recalled, the prison doctor used the dye not as treatment, but as a weapon of sexual humiliation — painting his body and then summoning others to look.

“Here he drew a man’s penis,” Shyshko said, pointing to his face. “Here he drew breasts. And here,” he added, pointing to his back, “he wrote the inscription ‘fuck here,’ with an arrow pointing to my buttocks. … Then he called someone else to come in and see what was happening.”

Shyshko, a 43-year-old special services officer, had been captured in the first weeks of Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022, and ended up spending about a year in Colony No. 7 before being freed in a prisoner exchange.
snippet: In the summer of 2023, a virulent scabies epidemic broke out in Prison Colony No. 7, deep in the Russian heartland.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Sinaloa Cartel Co-Founder 'El Mayo' Zambada Sentenced to Life in US Prison
content:
A court in the United States sent on Monday Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada Garcia, the 77-year-old co-founder of the Sinaloa Cartel, behind bars for the rest of his life without parole and ordered the forfeiture of his fortune worth $15 billion, the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) announced.

The sentence, handed down by the U.S. District Judge Brian M. Cogan, follows Zambada’s guilty plea to racketeering and running a continuing criminal enterprise.

The ruling closes the book on a decades-long manhunt that ended with a dramatic arrest in July 2024. Zambada, who previously had a $15 million U.S. bounty on his head, was taken into custody near El Paso, Texas, after landing in a small aircraft alongside Joaquin Guzman Lopez, the son of fellow cartel co-founder Joaquin “El Chapo” Guzman. The Mexican government stated at the time that it did not participate in the operation.

“Ismael Zambada Garcia spent nearly four decades poisoning American communities to make billions of dollars in profit and ordering the murders of anyone who stood in his way,” Joseph Nocella, Jr., U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of New York, said in the DOJ statement. “Today, that chapter closes for good.”
snippet: A court in the United States sent on Monday Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada Garcia, the 77-year-old co-founder of the Sinaloa Cartel, behind bars for the rest of his life without parole and ordered the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Co-Founder of Controversial Spyware Firm Had Israeli Diplomatic Passport
content:
Dozens of countries have deployed the powerful and controversial spyware tool Pegasus to surveil domestic and foreign targets. 

Developed by Israeli firm NSO Group, the spyware has the capacity to gather a target’s most private information: it can infiltrate a cellphone to access its files, photos, and browsing history, and turn its microphone and camera into listening and seeing devices for government intelligence agents. 

While Pegasus was supposed to be used to fight terrorism and serious crime, a 2021 investigation by a consortium of investigative media outlets, coordinated by the Paris-based nonprofit Forbidden Stories and including OCCRP, revealed how the software had been sold to autocratic governments around the world and used to spy on politicians, activists, and journalists.

NSO Group has long maintained that it does not operate the spyware tool on behalf of its clients, has no access to target data, and operates independently of the Israeli government. While the Israeli Defense Ministry must approve sales of Pegasus to foreign governments, NSO Group and Israeli officials have denied the existence of any formal links between the state and NSO Group.
snippet: Dozens of countries have deployed the powerful and controversial spyware tool Pegasus to surveil domestic and foreign targets.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: EU Lawmakers Back Alumina Sanctions Push
content:
Members of the European Parliament on Wednesday backed a non-binding call for a ban on alumina exports to Russia, increasing pressure on Ireland and EU member states to close what critics describe as a loophole in sanctions against Moscow.

The amendment, adopted by a show of hands during a vote in Strasbourg, called for stronger EU measures against Russia, including a “full ban” on alumina exports to Russia. The wider resolution on Ukraine and sanctions passed comfortably, with 460 MEPs voting in favor, 136 against, and 59 abstaining, according to The Irish Times.

The vote is not legally binding. EU sanctions are proposed by the High Representative for Foreign Affairs and require unanimous approval from all 27 member states. But the Parliament’s backing adds political pressure ahead of future sanctions talks, after alumina exports to Russia were left out of the EU’s latest sanctions package.

Dutch MEP Thijs Reuten, who co-authored the amendment, told OCCRP the vote reflected a European political consensus that “all countries need to do their part” and put “additional pressure on Member States to overcome short-term national interests.”
snippet: Members of the European Parliament on Wednesday backed a non-binding call for a ban on alumina exports to Russia, increasing pressure on Ireland and EU member states to close what critics describe as
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Armenian Tycoon and Opposition Leader Detained in Fraud Probe; Lions, Tiger Seized
content:
An Armenian court on Tuesday ordered billionaire businessman and opposition leader Gagik Tsarukyan to be held in pre-trial detention for two months on charges of large-scale fraud and money laundering.

Tsarukyan, long one of Armenia's most influential and flamboyant public figures, is as well known for his eccentric wealth as his politics. He made international headlines in 2023 for launching plans to erect a massive statue of Jesus Christ in Armenia, and videos of his private residential menagerie—where he kept exotic wild cats—frequently went viral online.

Now, those exotic animals and his massive business empire are at the center of a sweeping criminal crackdown. Tsarukyan was arrested Monday after security forces raided his private mansion and more than 70 corporate locations, including his sports complexes, a brandy factory, and the AraratCement plant.

According to Armenia’s Investigative Committee, Tsarukyan orchestrated a criminal group between 2022 and 2024 that used joint logistics and freight ventures to defraud Iranian businesses out of 52 vehicles, machinery, and fuel worth $21.07 million.
snippet: An Armenian court on Tuesday ordered billionaire businessman and opposition leader Gagik Tsarukyan to be held in pre-trial detention for two months on charges of large-scale fraud and money
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Venezuelan Housing Program Scrutinized After Quakes Brought Down Buildings
content:
One month before his re-election in October 2012, Venezuela’s then-president Hugo Chávez delivered on a campaign promise to provide social housing: 93 residential buildings in the coastal town of Playa Grande had been constructed in less than a year.

Less than two weeks ago, residents of that community became victims of the devastating back-to-back earthquakes that killed more than 3,500 people, with the number expected to rise. Satellite images and photos from the ground show that some of the buildings collapsed entirely, while others were partially damaged.

Damaged buildings in the Hugo Chávez housing complex in Playa Grande, Venezuela, after twin earthquakes on June 24, 2026. (Credit: Armando.info)

Experts who analyzed the photos questioned whether the development had been built according to Venezuela’s earthquake safety guidelines. The number of people living in the community also far exceeded the government's recommendation for population density given the level of seismic risk, reporters found.

The housing project at Playa Grande is not the only one to face scrutiny in the aftermath the twin quakes, which struck less than a minute apart just after 6 p.m. on June 24
snippet: One month before his re-election in October 2012, Venezuela’s then-president Hugo Chávez delivered on a campaign promise to provide social housing: 93 residential buildings in the coastal town of
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: China: Former Official Sentenced to Death in $325 Million Bribery Case
content:
A Chinese court has sentenced a former senior official to death for accepting more than 2.21 billion yuan ($325 million) in bribes over a 30-year period, according to a statement from the Changzhou Intermediate People's Court in Jiangsu province.

The court found 69-year-old Yang Youlin guilty of bribery, embezzlement, money laundering, and abuse of power. The offenses were committed while he held various administrative positions from 1993 to 2023, including his role as deputy director of an economic zone in the eastern city of Nanjing.

In addition to the death sentence, the court stripped Yang of his political rights for life and ordered the confiscation of all his personal property and assets.

The ruling marks one of the most severe penalties handed down amid Beijing's ongoing anti-corruption campaign. While Chinese courts frequently issue suspended death sentences that are eventually commuted to life imprisonment, the scale of Yang's bribery resulted in the death penalty. This follows the execution last December of Bai Tianhui, the former general manager of a state-owned investment firm who was executed after being convicted of accepting 1.1 billion yuan ($156 million) in bribes.
snippet: A Chinese court has sentenced a former senior official to death for accepting more than 2.21 billion yuan ($325 million) in bribes over a 30-year period, according to a statement from the Changzhou
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Spanish Authorities Confiscate a Record Amount of Ecstasy
content:
Spanish and Dutch authorities have detained 15 suspects and seized nearly a metric ton of MDMA in what officials called the largest bust of the synthetic drug in European history.

On Tuesday, Spanish police announced that investigators confiscated 947 kilograms of the drug—commonly known as ecstasy or molly—during coordinated raids across 11 locations in Spain and one in the Netherlands.

The narcotics were found hidden inside industrial machinery bound for Ghana. Authorities say the syndicate planned to route the shipment through West Africa before delivering it to South America, where the MDMA was to be exchanged for cocaine.

The joint operation began in early 2025 as an investigation into a sophisticated network based in southern Spain that specialized in international drug trafficking and money laundering.
snippet: Spanish and Dutch authorities have detained 15 suspects and seized nearly a metric ton of MDMA in what officials called the largest bust of the synthetic drug in European history.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: French Appeals Court Upholds Marine Le Pen’s Conviction but Clears Her to Run for Office
content:
A French appeals court on Tuesday upheld the embezzlement conviction of far-right leader Marine Le Pen and her party over the misuse of European Parliament funds, but adjusted her sentence to preserve her right to run for future office.

The Paris Court of Appeal found Le Pen, 11 other individuals, and her National Rally party—formerly the National Front—guilty of diverting European funds intended to pay European parliamentary assistants to staff working for the national political party in France. 

The funds were diverted for 11 years and the financial damage suffered by the European Parliament was assessed at 2.8 million euros ($3.2 million). The defendants were ordered to reimburse the amount to compensate the institution.

The court noted the fraud was systematic, operating between 2004 and 2016. Defendants knew the employment contracts "did not correspond to reality" and allowed the party to illegally finance its operations using European money, the court said.
snippet: A French appeals court on Tuesday upheld the embezzlement conviction of far-right leader Marine Le Pen and her party over the misuse of European Parliament funds, but adjusted her sentence to preserve
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Inside the European Gambling Operations of a Wanted Turkish Businessman
content:
Fedlan Kılıçaslan portrays himself as a business visionary and philanthropist. Since relocating to Warsaw from Ukraine four years ago, the 44-year-old Turkish entrepreneur has made a splash in the Polish capital, driving a custom-built Ferrari with vanity plates, buying one of the city’s priciest apartments.

But driving his wealth are online casinos that have triggered criminal investigations across multiple borders.

A joint investigation by OCCRP, Frontstory.pl, TVN24, and the investigative portals NGL.media (Ukraine) and CIReN (Cyprus) reveals that Kılıçaslan’s Poland-registered “IT company” has provided telemarketing for blacklisted online gambling sites. 

Now, authorities are taking action. 

Polish authorities are looking into Kılıçaslan and several related entities in connection with a money-laundering investigation, a spokesperson with the Warsaw District Prosecutor's Office confirmed. The official declined to answer if Kılıçaslan is formally a suspect in this case and provide further details, citing the ongoing nature of the investigation. 

Authorities in Spain, meanwhile, have banned Kılıçaslan from leaving the country following his arrest on unrelated sexual assault charges there.
snippet: Fedlan Kılıçaslan portrays himself as a business visionary and philanthropist. Since relocating to Warsaw from Ukraine four years ago, the 44-year-old Turkish entrepreneur has made a splash in the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Global Anti-Trafficking Sweep Rescues Over 2,000 Victims and Nets 1,024 Arrests
content:
A global crackdown on human trafficking across 59 countries resulted in the arrest of 1,024 suspects and the detection of 2,070 potential victims, Europol stated Monday.

Coordinated alongside Frontex and INTERPOL between June 8 and 12, Operation "Global Chain" mobilized over 40,000 law enforcement officers to target criminal networks involved in sexual exploitation, forced criminality, and forced begging. Authorities checked over half a million individuals, opened 465 new criminal investigations, and identified 334 suspects directly tied to human trafficking.

According to the agency, the vast majority of the victims were adult females, with 64.2 percent trafficked for sexual exploitation. That rate jumped to 86.4 percent for the 162 identified underage victims, who authorities noted are often exploited by their own family members. Victims originated from 45 different countries—predominantly Colombia, Argentina, Venezuela, Nepal, and Moldova—highlighting the highly transnational nature of modern trafficking schemes.
snippet: A global crackdown on human trafficking across 59 countries resulted in the arrest of 1,024 suspects and the detection of 2,070 potential victims, Europol stated Monday.
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Serbian President Dismisses Plot Against Football Legend Vidić as Lawyer Files Criminal Complaint
content:
Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić downplayed recent revelations that the former head of the country’s football association plotted to violently intimidate Manchester United legend Nemanja Vidić for criticizing his work, even as state prosecutors announced they are seeking police records to take action on the case.

The public fallout follows a June 30 investigation published by OCCRP and its Serbian partner KRIK. Using leaked messages from the encrypted Sky ECC app, reporters revealed that Slaviša Kokeza, former president of the Football Association of Serbia (FSS), discussed hiring criminals in 2020 to track and assault Vidić and his supporters after the football star publicly criticized the association.

The investigation also revealed Kokeza planned retaliation against footballers Nemanja Matić, Danko Lazović, and Dragan Mrđa because they had publicly supported Vidić. The messages further showed that Kokeza sought to lobby influential Red Star Belgrade supporters to back his position at the FSS, and that some of his contacts were also in communication with the notorious criminal Veljko Belivuk.
snippet: Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić downplayed recent revelations that the former head of the country’s football association plotted to violently intimidate Manchester United legend Nemanja Vidić for
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Iraq: PM Announces Rewards for Whistleblowers as Anti-Corruption Crackdown Widens
content:
Iraq's Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi announced Sunday that citizens who report assets and public funds obtained through corruption will receive “substantial financial rewards.”

In a statement reported by the state news agency INA, the prime minister’s office called on citizens to “report assets and public funds obtained through corruption, in order to contribute to their discovery, recovery, and return to the state.”

The announcement comes as the government intensifies an anti-graft campaign that began in late May with the arrest of Adnan Al-Jumaili, Iraq's deputy minister of oil and refining affairs, alongside several associates. That initial operation uncovered hundreds of millions of dollars in embezzled funds, OCCRP previously reported. Authorities have since detained dozens of senior officials and politicians in what state media described as one of the largest anti-graft sweeps in recent history.

Separately, the Iraqi Ministry of Justice announced it has recovered more than $25 million in looted public funds over the past two years.
snippet: Iraq's Prime Minister Ali Al-Zaidi announced Sunday that citizens who report assets and public funds obtained through corruption will receive “substantial financial rewards.”
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Former Kyrgyz Security Chief Sentenced for Trying to Violently Seize Power
content:
A Kyrgyz court has found former security chief Kamchybek Tashiev guilty of attempting to violently seize power from his longtime ally and friend, President Sadyr Japarov. The conviction stems from an incident earlier this year when several high-ranking officials published an open letter calling for early presidential elections.

Intriguingly, Tashiev neither signed nor published the document, and his defense team maintains it remains entirely unclear why the court concluded he was part of a conspiracy. In the petition—now widely known as the "Letter of 75"—the authors praised Japarov’s achievements but argued that the country nevertheless required early election.

President Japarov interpreted the public call as a coordinated coup attempt and immediately purged the signatories holding public office. Tashiev was dismissed from his dual roles as head of the State Committee for National Security (GKNB) and deputy chairman of the Cabinet of Ministers. Along with several subordinates, Tashiev was charged and convicted, though his lawyer noted that the prosecution failed to specify what overt acts the former security chief actually committed.
snippet: A Kyrgyz court has found former security chief Kamchybek Tashiev guilty of attempting to violently seize power from his longtime ally and friend, President Sadyr Japarov. The conviction stems from an
creator: OCCRP

title: Companies Awarded Security Contracts in Mexico's Largest Airport Had Ties to Alleged Gun Traffickers
content:
By the time the World Cup ends this month, Mexico City International Airport will have welcomed an estimated 5.5-million extra passengers, with football fans from around the globe converging on the country.

Many of those arrivals are passing through inspection points secured by companies with links to two men authorities suspect of providing weapons to drug cartels.

OCCRP media partner Mexicans Against Corruption and Impunity (MCCI) has found that the two companies — SERPROSEP SA de CV and Armour King SA de CV — were awarded three-year contracts for airport security last year. 

The registry document dated 2022 for SERPROSEP lists Jorge Enrique Alberts Ponce and Alfonso Sainz de la Maza Zúñiga as its co-owners.

Alberts is a subject of a November 2025 federal arrest warrant, while Sainz de la Maza is named in the investigation as his alleged associate. Sanz de la Maza Zúñiga is not wanted by authorities.
snippet: By the time the World Cup ends this month, Mexico City International Airport will have welcomed an estimated 5.5-million extra passengers, with football fans from around the globe converging on the
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Businessman Who Allegedly Posed as CIA Agent Wooed Indonesian President and His Brother
content:
A businessman who allegedly posed as a CIA agent cultivated a relationship with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto — and secured preliminary agreements from Indonesia’s government to purchase tens of billions of dollars worth of fighter jets and other equipment.

The businessman, Gaurav Srivastava, did not respond to requests for comment on his Indonesian activities. On his website, Srivastava stated that accusations that he pretended to be a CIA agent were “gross fabrications” spread by his former business partner, Niels Troost, who was sanctioned by the U.K., European Union and Switzerland for trading Russian oil. 

The sanctions have since been lifted by the E.U. and Switzerland. 

Civil lawsuits filed by Troost in California and the Southern District of New York include references to recorded phone calls he collected as evidence in which Srivastava allegedly claimed to work for the CIA. 

Srivastava joined Prabowo, then defence minister, in high-level meetings in Washington D.C. and Jakarta in 2020 where military procurement was discussed. He received three “Letters of Intention to Purchase” the same year.
snippet: A businessman who allegedly posed as a CIA agent cultivated a relationship with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto — and secured preliminary agreements from Indonesia’s government to purchase tens
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Libyan Authorities Weaponize Xenophobic Rhetoric to Justify Mass Migrant Expulsions, HRW Warns
content:
Despite competing for power, Libya's rival authorities in the east and west have united in stoking anti-migrant rhetoric and launching a violent campaign of mass detention and expulsions against migrants and refugees, Human Rights Watch (HRW) said Thursday.

According to the rights watchdog, both the western-based administration in Tripoli and its eastern rival have deployed “incendiary rhetoric” and pursued a crackdown on migrants, asylum seekers, and refugees, fueling a wave of xenophobia and triggering anti-migrant protests.

“Rival Libyan authorities have united in fueling xenophobic protests and subjecting migrants to mass arrests, arbitrary detention in inhumane conditions, and collective expulsions,” said Hanan Salah, associate Middle East and North Africa director at HRW. “With thousands of people detained and at risk of expulsion, the scale of the abuses, and the urgency of stopping them, could not be clearer.”
snippet: Despite competing for power, Libya's rival authorities in the east and west have united in stoking anti-migrant rhetoric and launching a violent campaign of mass detention and expulsions against
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Zelensky Demands Severing of Russian Supply Chains; Targets Irish Refinery
content:
Before rushing back home from Dublin amid news of an imminent, massive Russian strike on Kyiv, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged allies to sever global supply chains feeding  Russian militarization.

The Ukrainian leader raised this issue in Ireland where a factory is currently under investigation for allegedly supplying exporting critical raw material to Russian smelters who feed into the aluminum supply chain of the country’s arms makers.

“Every ton of raw material that ends in Russia is used against us. I do hope whatever we are doing now, we are cutting every possibility, every sphere for Russia to build up, to militarize itself,” he said.

“When their economy is shrinking, we can see that immediately,” he told reporters. “Just as it is shrinking, Russia’s ability to perform massive attacks decreases, so we are really interested in the results of this investigation.”
snippet: Before rushing back home from Dublin amid news of an imminent, massive Russian strike on Kyiv, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged allies to sever global supply chains feeding Russian militarization.
creator: OCCRP
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Finnish Court Jails Businessman Over Exports to Russia
content:
A Finnish court sentenced the chief executive of a transport company to three years and eight months in prison for exporting trucks and trailers to Russia in violation of EU sanctions, the Finnish public service broadcaster, Yle, reported Wednesday. 

The South Karelia District Court found that Risto Riihimäki, head of Lappeenranta-based Idän Liikenteenvälitys IL Oy, played a central role in sending 164 trucks and trailers to Russia in 2022 and 2023, despite restrictions imposed after Moscow’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine.The company, which now operates as Rent ja Kalusto Oy, had declared that the vehicles were bound for companies registered in Turkey and Kazakhstan and were only transiting through Russia, according to Yle. 

The court found that the vehicles in fact ended up in Russia, rather than merely passing through the country. It also ordered the company to forfeit about 600,000 euros ($685,134) in criminal proceeds and property worth 6 million euros ($6.85 million) to the state. Riihimäki denied wrongdoing, saying he believed the vehicles were going to Kazakhstan. The ruling can be appealed.
snippet: A Finnish court sentenced the chief executive of a transport company to three years and eight months in prison for exporting trucks and trailers to Russia in violation of EU sanctions, the Finnish
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Liberian Senate Grills Police About $19M Airport Cocaine Bust
content:
Following widespread outcry in Liberia about a lack of apparent progress in their investigation of a large airport cocaine bust, senior police officials appealed for patience and promised imminent arrests under intense questioning in a public hearing at the country’s senate Wednesday.

“We will make arrests. We have elevated people from persons of interest to suspects and we will make arrests in the coming days,” Gregory Coleman, Inspector General of the Liberia National Police told the full plenary of the Liberian Senate.

The senate called the special public hearing with Liberia’s joint security and national security advisers as public scrutiny grows following the seizure of 237.6 kg of cocaine at Roberts International Airport last month.

The Liberia Drug Enforcement Agency (LDEA) initially hailed the June 8 confiscation valued at roughly $19 million at Roberts International Airport as a breakthrough in Liberia's fight against transnational drug trafficking.
snippet: Following widespread outcry in Liberia about a lack of apparent progress in their investigation of a large airport cocaine bust, senior police officials appealed for patience and promised imminent
creator: Rodney D. Sieh
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Most-Wanted Southern Cone Drug Trafficking Suspect Gets Trial Date in the US
content:
A U.S. District Judge in Virginia on Wednesday set a trial date for early 2027 for accused Southern Cone drug trafficker Sebastián Marset, rejecting attempts by prosecutors to push it back to March, a year after his arrest.

Marset’s lawyer Robert Feitel asked the judge to hold a trial for Marset as early as November, a move prosecutors complained would not give them time to line up visas for foreign witnesses. District Judge Rossie D. Alston split the difference and set a date for January 11, 2027, anticipating two or more weeks for what he deemed a complex case.

The move puts pressure on prosecutor Anthony T. Aminoff, who heads the district’s narcotics and money laundering for the Justice Department. He told the judge he needed to obtain visas for law enforcement partners from Belgium and Colombia who participated in the investigation, which the court heard Wednesday actually began in 2021.
snippet: A U.S. District Judge in Virginia on Wednesday set a trial date for early 2027 for accused Southern Cone drug trafficker Sebastián Marset, rejecting attempts by prosecutors to push it back to March, a
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: EU Prosecutor Raids Far-Right European Parliament Group Over Alleged Fraud
content:
European prosecutors have carried out raids across multiple countries as part of an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of EU funds by a now-defunct far-right group in the European Parliament. 

The European Public Prosecutor’s Office (EPPO) confirmed to OCCRP on Wednesday that it “conducted investigative measures in France and other European countries as part of an ongoing investigation into the use of EU funds by a former political group of the European Parliament between 2019 and 2024.”

The Luxembourg-based prosecutor's office added that it would not disclose further details “in order not to endanger the outcome of the proceedings.”

French daily Le Monde, which first reported the story, said the raids were underway across France, Spain, Italy, and Belgium. The probe centers on suspicions of financial misconduct within the former Identity and Democracy (ID) group. The defunct European Parliament faction's prominent members included Marine Le Pen's French far-right National Rally (RN) and the Alternative for Germany (AfD), which was expelled from the group in mid-2024.
snippet: European prosecutors have carried out raids across multiple countries as part of an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of EU funds by a now-defunct far-right group in the European
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Bulgaria Detains 8 in EU Subsidy Fraud Probe
content:
Bulgarian authorities have detained eight suspects, including a public official, following an investigation into an organized crime group accused of fraudulently securing nearly 1 million euro ($1.17 million) in EU employment subsidies, the European Public Prosecutor’s Office (EPPO) announced.

According to investigators, the network used shell companies to apply for funding under Bulgaria’s Human Resources Development Program for 2021–2027. The suspects allegedly submitted falsified data and fabricated fictitious employment contracts to meet strict eligibility requirements.

During the crackdown, officers from Bulgaria’s General Directorate for Combating Organized Crime executed eight search warrants, seizing documents and other critical evidence.
snippet: Bulgarian authorities have detained eight suspects, including a public official, following an investigation into an organized crime group accused of fraudulently securing nearly 1 million euro ($1.17
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Malta Tycoon Stands Trial For Journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 Assassination
content:
Nearly nine years after a car bomb killed the investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, the jury trial of Yorgen Fenech, the businessman accused of ordering her assassination, has begun on Wednesday in Valletta.

The trial, one of the most consequential in modern Maltese history, is expected to last several weeks. 

On 16th October 2017 at 14:58, journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, 53, was murdered in a car bomb near her home which lies on the outskirts of the city Mosta. The bomb was placed under the driver’s seat of the vehicle. It was detonated remotely by SMS.

Five individuals, George Degiorgio, Alfred Degiorgio, Vince Muscat, Robert Agius and Jamie Vella, have all been found guilty for their roles in the murder. Meanwhile, Yorgen Fenech, one of Malta's most powerful businessmen, denies any involvement and has pleaded not guilty. 

Fenech was arrested on 20th November 2019, after authorities intercepted his yacht as he allegedly attempted to flee Malta by sea. Ten days later, he was arraigned before the Court of Magistrates, charged with complicity in the murder of Caruana Galizia and with promoting, organizing, or financing a criminal organization under Maltese law.
snippet: Nearly nine years after a car bomb killed the investigative journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia, the jury trial of Yorgen Fenech, the businessman accused of ordering her assassination, has begun on
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Documentary Raises New Concerns About Protection Measures of Musk’s AI Grok
content:
Grok, the large language model (LLM) developed and operated by Elon Musk’s xAI, is facing intense scrutiny following an international investigation revealing that the chatbot repeatedly bypassed its own guardrails to engage in conspiracies and violent fantasies.

In conversations with reporters, Grok promoted anti-Semitic conspiracies, assisted with a suicide note, and even generated explicit murder and child sexual assault fantasies. 

In a documentary released this week, OCCRP’s Swedish media partner TV4 revealed how text conversations with Grok could, over time, cross into dangerous territory. 

New laws have been introduced in many places to criminalize child sexual abuse material (CSAM) images produced using AI, and LLM companies face a rising number of lawsuits tied to users committing suicide or violence against others. But the new findings raise questions over the lack of legislation to police disturbing conversation topics produced by chatbots.
snippet: Grok, the large language model (LLM) developed and operated by Elon Musk’s xAI, is facing intense scrutiny following an international investigation revealing that the chatbot repeatedly bypassed its
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Iraq Intensifies Anti-Graft Drive with Dozens of High-Profile Arrests, Asset Seizures
content:
A sweeping anti-corruption campaign led by Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has resulted in the arrest of dozens of senior officials and lawmakers, drawing praise from parliamentary watchdogs who also warn that the drive must cut deeper to dismantle the country’s entrenched patronage networks.

Taha Difai, the head of Iraq’s parliamentary Committee for Integrity, cautioned in an interview with OCCRP that the high-profile arrests and asset seizures would ultimately yield little long-term impact unless investigations targeted the powerful political factions that have dominated public spending for more than two decades.

“We support the government's measures to combat corruption, pursue corrupt officials and recover public funds. These are positive steps,” Difai said. “But what concerns us is that the theft of public money is not limited to this small group.
snippet: A sweeping anti-corruption campaign led by Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has resulted in the arrest of dozens of senior officials and lawmakers, drawing praise from parliamentary watchdogs who also warn
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Guilty Plea in New York in Massive $67 Million Identity Theft and Laundering Plot
content:
A Vietnamese national pleaded guilty before a New York court to conspiracy to commit identity theft as part of a transnational scheme to launder $67 million using data stolen from U.S. citizens, according to a statement from the Department of Justice.

Le Van Hung, a permanent U.S. resident, was extradited from South Korea in November 2024 to stand trial for his role in the scheme that involved the stolen identities of thousands of U.S. residents and benefited a New York-based multinational media company.

As part of his plea, Le Van Hung agreed to forfeit at least $67 million. He is scheduled to be sentenced on September 29, 2026. He could get maximum five years in prison.
snippet: A Vietnamese national pleaded guilty before a New York court to conspiracy to commit identity theft as part of a transnational scheme to launder $67 million using data stolen from U.S. citizens,

title: French Authorities Search Suspected VAT Fraud Network
content:
French authorities searched premises in the Paris region and questioned six people as part of an investigation into a suspected large-scale VAT carousel fraud that allegedly cost the EU around 13 million euros ($13.9 million), the European Public Prosecutor’s Office said. 

Carousel fraud is a cross-border tax scam where fraudsters import goods VAT-free, sell them domestically with VAT included, and then vanish with the collected tax before exporting the same goods to repeat the cycle.

The probe also concerns suspected money laundering and participation in a criminal organization involving 26 French companies, most of them based in Île-de-France. Investigators believe the transnational scheme relied on short-lived shell companies with no real business activity, which allegedly used at least 80 bank and payment accounts to move around 160 million euros ($171 million) in less than two years. 

During six searches on June 25, authorities seized documents, digital evidence, a luxury car and about 60,000 euros ($64,000) in cash.
snippet: French authorities searched premises in the Paris region and questioned six people as part of an investigation into a suspected large-scale VAT carousel fraud that allegedly cost the EU around 13
sourceDescription: Feed, delivered straight to you. Well, your RSS reader anyway.

title: Leaked Messages Reveal Former Serbian Football Chief Discussed Violently Intimidating Manchester United Legend Nemanja Vidić
content:
After Serbia failed to qualify for the European Football Championship in November 2020, former national team defender and Manchester United captain Nemanja Vidić publicly condemned the state of the country’s football association, describing a corrupt system in which executives and agents were profiting at the expense of the sport itself. 

Behind the scenes, Serbia’s football chief feared the celebrated footballer might be coming for his job — and began discussing a vicious response.

Encrypted messages obtained by OCCRP and KRIK show a chat account that law enforcement identified as used by Slaviša Kokeza, then-head of the Football Association of Serbia (FSS), sought to silence Vidić with the help of contacts connected to Serbian criminals and football hooligans. 

The messages detail an apparent plot to threaten Vidić, pressure him to stay out of football politics, and physically attack other former national team players who had supported him.

The conversations — whose details support the conclusion that Kokeza was behind the account — took place over the now defunct platform Sky ECC, which was broken into by European police in 2021.
snippet: After Serbia failed to qualify for the European Football Championship in November 2020, former national team defender and Manchester United captain Nemanja Vidić publicly condemned the state of the

title: U.S. Adds Narcoterror Charge Against Uruguayan Trafficking Suspect
content:
U.S. prosecutors have filed a second superseding indictment against Sebastián Marset, adding narcoterrorism and cocaine-trafficking charges just days after the accused Uruguayan claimed in a letter to a federal judge that federal agents had pressured his mother to hand over the access keys to his cryptocurrency wallet.

The indictment charges Marset with four counts: narcoterrorism conspiracy, conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute cocaine aboard a vessel subject to U.S. jurisdiction, money laundering conspiracy, and money laundering.

Prosecutors allege that Marset led a South America-based drug trafficking and money laundering organization also known as the Primer Cartel Uruguayo, or First Uruguayan Cartel. The indictment says the group moved “ton quantities of cocaine” from South America to locations around the world, especially Europe.
snippet: U.S. prosecutors have filed a second superseding indictment against Sebastián Marset, adding narcoterrorism and cocaine-trafficking charges just days after the accused Uruguayan claimed in a letter to
